title: Group Travel in Otsuka: A Retro Tram-Town Base Near Sugamo and Ikebukuro
excerpt: Why Plat House Otsuka's old-fashioned tram-town setting makes it an easy, affordable base near Sugamo and Ikebukuro.
contentHtml:
Otsuka is one of the few parts of Tokyo where the Toden Arakawa tram line still rattles down the street, giving the neighborhood a genuinely old-fashioned, unhurried feel. It's a short trip from both Sugamo's shopping streets and Ikebukuro's bigger city energy.

A Private House Near the Station

Plat House Otsuka is a full house rental within walking distance of Otsuka Station on the JR Yamanote Line, giving your group a shared kitchen, living space, and laundry facilities rather than splitting up across separate hotel rooms.

Comfortable for Longer Stays

Netflix and a Nintendo Switch cover the evenings, and the kitchen and washing machine make a multi-day stay easy to manage without eating out for every meal.

Trams, Temples, and Gardens

The Toden Arakawa tram line is worth riding at least once, and Sugamo's Jizo-dori shopping street — sometimes called 'Harajuku for grandmothers' — is a short trip away. Ikebukuro's Sunshine City is close by for shopping and entertainment, and the Kyu-Furukawa Gardens and Rikugien offer some of Tokyo's finest traditional landscaping nearby.

Book Direct and Save

Booking directly with Plat House Otsuka beats typical OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days before check-in.

Check Availability & Book Now
